**Method/coverage note**: obtained via direct file read of the struct literal (grep to locate the boundaries, then `Read` the full 8703–8813 range), not via LSP `documentSymbol` — clangd's outline for a struct-literal initializer wouldn't expose per-field function bindings usefully, so a plain read was the more reliable primitive here. Verified field count with `awk`/grep (98 `.field = ` lines total → 96 function pointers + `.name`/`.desc`). Fields inside the `CONFIG_TDLS`, `ANDROID_P2P`, `ANDROID`/`ANDROID_LIB_STUB`, and `CONFIG_MESH` `#ifdef` blocks are only active in builds with those macros defined — they're part of the source struct literal but may not be compiled into the single active `compile_commands.json` configuration; that single-config caveat doesn't affect this static text-based enumeration, just any LSP-based cross-check of those specific lines. Out of the 136 total fields in `wpa_driver_ops`, this backend fills 96 (~71%); the remaining ~40 are left as `NULL`/unset.
